notes: Recording police in public is generally allowed in France, but sharing identifiable footage can be restricted under privacy and security laws.
Home CCTV is legal in France when used to monitor your own property, but strict privacy rules apply if cameras capture public areas or neighbours.
Employee monitoring is allowed in France under strict conditions, including transparency, necessity, and respect for worker privacy.
Recording private conversations without consent from all parties is illegal in France.
Recording video in public is allowed in France, but sharing or publishing identifiable footage without consent can violate privacy laws.
Recording phone calls without the consent of all parties is illegal in France.
GPS tracking is restricted in France and generally requires consent or a clear legal basis, especially when tracking individuals.
Drone use is legal in France under strict aviation rules, but using drones to monitor people can raise privacy and data protection concerns.
Business CCTV is allowed in France under strict conditions, including necessity, transparency, and compliance with data protection laws.
Hidden cameras are generally illegal in France when used to secretly record individuals without their knowledge.
